findings.resolution: CP7-D accepted the discovery and armored-rejection rows as generic ArmorClass target-admission route-shape evidence and the duration-expiry row as generic ArmorClass active-effect lifecycle route-shape evidence. The evidence is observed through BattleEntrypointTrace.route_events and does not claim full selected Mage Armor semantics.
findings.summary: Mage Armor discovery, armored rejection, and duration expiry need generic route-owner evidence rather than projection-only comparisons.

findings.resolution: Added generic ReactionSpell fills for armor-class interruption and failed-save damage facts, then routed accepted rows through production discovery from explicit reaction-window substrates; production code does not dispatch on selected spell identity.
findings.summary: Reaction branches could have been routed by selected spell name.

rounds.summary: Checked RAW/QNT traceability, domain language, architecture/connascence, adapter quarantine, state ownership, and report honesty after FU01E route implementation.

Convergence pass after accepting CP7-D Mage Armor target-admission and active-effect lifecycle rows as route-shape evidence and confirming accepted AC/reaction rows use reducer entrypoint route evidence, with ReactionSpell rows discovered from production reaction-window state; no reasonable findings remain.
